title: executive_tabletop
description: The CEO, CFO, General Counsel, and board members participate in a ransomware tabletop exercise focused on business continuity, communications, and governance decisions — with no technical jargon — testing executive decision-making under a cyber crisis.

timeline.title: Scenario Briefing
timeline.description: Facilitator briefs executives: core business systems encrypted, recovery 48–72 hours, ransom demand received.

timeline.title: First Decision Point: Ransom
timeline.description: Executives must decide whether to pay or not within the first 30 minutes of the exercise.

timeline.title: Communication Decisions
timeline.description: Board discusses external communications: customers, media, regulators, and investors.

timeline.title: Business Continuity Choices
timeline.description: CFO presents financial impact projections. COO presents manual fallback options.

timeline.title: Governance and Accountability
timeline.description: Board asks accountability questions: Who knew? What controls existed? Were they adequate?

timeline.title: Lessons Identified
timeline.description: Facilitator debriefs. Key governance and communication gaps identified.

node-1.title: Ransomware — The CEO Is in the Room
node-1.situation: You are the facilitator of an executive tabletop exercise. The scenario: it is 8 a.m. on a Monday and your core ERP and CRM systems are encrypted by ransomware. IT estimates 48–72 hours to restore from backup. The attacker is demanding £500,000 in cryptocurrency within 24 hours. The CFO has calculated £2 million per day of lost revenue. The CEO is chairing this session. What is the first governance decision they must make?
node-1.question: What is the CEO's first decision?

options.label: A
options.text: The CEO convenes an emergency board sub-committee with Legal, the CFO, and the CISO (or equivalent technical advisor) to formally authorise the response strategy. The first governance decision is: activate the crisis management plan and establish decision-making authority and thresholds. This is not a technical decision — it is a governance one.

options.label: B
options.text: The CEO asks the IT team for a recommendation on whether to pay the ransom and defers the decision entirely to IT.

options.label: C
options.text: The CEO immediately authorises payment of the ransom to minimise business disruption, without consulting Legal or the board.

node-2.title: To Pay or Not to Pay — Board-Level Decision
node-2.situation: Legal has advised that payment may violate OFAC sanctions if the ransomware group is a designated entity. The CFO notes that 48-hour downtime at £2M/day equals £4M — eight times the ransom. Cyber insurance may cover the ransom but the insurer requires police notification before payment. The CISO advises the backups are intact and restoration is feasible. The board must decide.
node-2.question: How does the board decide?

options.label: A
options.text: The board decides not to pay: (1) Legal has identified sanctions risk; (2) backups are viable; (3) paying funds criminal organisations and does not guarantee decryption. Instead, authorise full backup restoration, engage the cyber insurer for coverage of restoration costs, notify law enforcement as required by the insurer, and instruct Communications to prepare external messaging.

options.label: B
options.text: Authorise payment immediately — the financial loss from downtime outweighs the ransom and the legal risk is theoretical.

node-3.title: Investor and Regulatory Communications
node-3.situation: The company is publicly listed. The breach may be material and require a market disclosure under securities regulations. GDPR notifications are required within 72 hours if personal data is affected. The General Counsel advises that the CEO must personally sign off on any public statements. A journalist is already calling the communications team.
node-3.question: How does the board manage communications?

options.label: A
options.text: The General Counsel leads: immediately assess whether a securities market disclosure is required (consult the company's securities lawyer). File the GDPR notification in parallel — this can be an initial notification updated later. Prepare a factual press statement approved by the CEO and Legal. Brief the board chair so they are aligned before any public disclosure.

options.label: B
options.text: Instruct the Communications team to issue a brief 'no comment' response to all media enquiries and defer all formal notifications until recovery is complete.

node-4.title: Board Accountability Questions
node-4.situation: During the tabletop, the board chair asks the CEO three accountability questions: (1) What security investments did the organisation make in the past 12 months? (2) Was this attack a known risk that had been reported to the board? (3) Is the organisation's current security posture adequate? These are real questions boards ask after incidents.
node-4.question: How should the CEO have prepared for these questions before an incident occurs?

options.label: A
options.text: The CEO should ensure: (1) a regular security risk report is presented to the board at least annually, including the current threat landscape and key risks; (2) material cyber risks are on the board risk register with agreed risk appetite; (3) the CISO (or equivalent) has direct board access; (4) security investments are presented in the context of risk reduction, not just IT spend.

options.label: B
options.text: Delegate all board cyber reporting to the IT department and assure the board that the professionals are handling it.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: The executive tabletop revealed key governance decisions: establishing crisis decision-making authority, rejecting ransom payment based on legal risk and viable backups, managing securities disclosure obligations, and identifying what boards need to know before an incident occurs. Executives now understand their roles in a cyber crisis and have identified governance improvements including a formal security risk reporting framework for the board.
node-5.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
